html:
Exclusive to:  

Cactus Flesh is a Vegetable Food Item from the Reign of Giants DLC. It can be harvested from Cactus at the cost of 6 . Eating it raw will cost the player 5 Sanity and 3 Health.

Cactus Flesh can be used as an ingredient in many Crock Pot recipes, but only Guacamole requires it specifically.

Feeding Cactus Flesh to Pigs will result in them producing Manure.

Depending on what type it is, wearing Armor while harvesting a cactus can reduce damage down to 1 . Since Cooked Cactus Flesh heals this amount, one can negate the harvesting damage entirely.

Since Cacti are unaffected by cold during Winter, one can easily harvest Cactus Flesh and eat it cooked to regain Sanity lost from the long nights, or used in lieu of farmed vegetables in Crock Pot recipes. Cactus is easily renewable in the Desert biomes which makes it a good base location.

Cooked Cactus Flesh

Cooked Cactus Flesh is acquired by cooking Cactus Flesh on a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It restores the same Hunger as its raw form, but does not hurt the player's Health and restores 15 instead.

Trivia

  • Despite the fact that the cooked cactus flesh does not harm the player upon consumption, and that the player has supposedly burned off the cactus spines by cooking it, it's sprite still depicts spines on it's surface.

Usage

Fillers cannot be Fruit.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“There are still some spines between me and a tasty meal.”
–Wilson, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“It still looks dangerous.”
–Willow, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Spiky plant meat.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“It's still dangerous.”
–Wendy, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“PROTECTIVE BARRIER REMAINS”
–WX-78, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“I'm not sure it's been prepared properly.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Would you put nails in your mouth?”
–Woodie, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“It would be unwise to put that in my mouth.”
–Maxwell, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Consuming this in its current state is inadvisable.”
–Wagstaff, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Swörd plant meat.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Haven't had enough, huh?”
–Webber, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Cactus flesh... d'you think that's vegetarian?”
–Walani, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“I hope it does not prickle going down.”
–Warly, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Dry and prickly, but 'tis still meat.”
–Woodlegs, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“'TIS POKEY YUM YUMS”
–Wilba, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Mmm... belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Still a little prickly.”
–Wheeler, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“It's got a sharp taste. Ha!”
–Winona, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“I don't want to eat anything prickly.”
–Wortox, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Ow! Still spiky.”
–Wurt, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Maybe I should take out the spines first.”
–Walter, when examining Cactus Flesh.
“Now to pick out all the spines. What a tedious vegetable.”
–Wanda, when examining Cactus Flesh.
Cactus Flesh is a Vegetable Food Item from the Reign of Giants DLC. It can be harvested from Cactus at the cost of 6 . Eating it raw will cost the player 5 Sanity and 3 Health.
Cactus Flesh can be used as an ingredient in many Crock Pot recipes, but only Guacamole requires it specifically.
Feeding Cactus Flesh to Pigs will result in them producing Manure.
Depending on what type it is, wearing Armor while harvesting a cactus can reduce damage down to 1 . Since Cooked Cactus Flesh heals this amount, one can negate the harvesting damage entirely.
Since Cacti are unaffected by cold during Winter, one can easily harvest Cactus Flesh and eat it cooked to regain Sanity lost from the long nights, or used in lieu of farmed vegetables in Crock Pot recipes. Cactus is easily renewable in the Desert biomes which makes it a good base location.
Cooked Cactus Flesh[edit | edit source]
“Grilled fruit of the desert.”
–Wilson, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“It seems fine now.”
–Willow, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Is safe now.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“I ripped all its spikes off.”
–Wendy, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“PROTECTIVE BARRIER REMOVED”
–WX-78, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Delightfully devoid of spines.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Much better.”
–Woodie, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Cactus comestible.”
–Maxwell, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“The cooking process has made it better for my constitution.”
–Wagstaff, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Töasted swörd plant meat.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Take that, pokey things!”
–Webber, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Looks pretty tasty now.”
–Walani, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Could use some tortillas and melted queso...”
–Warly, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Not bad when it ain't stickin' ye.”
–Woodlegs, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“WILBA EAT'TH NOW”
–Wilba, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Will eat it”
–Wormwood, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Bottom's up!”
–Wheeler, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“That seems a lot safer.”
–Winona, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“The prickles are gone, but I still don't want to eat it.”
–Wortox, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Mmmm, it eating time!”
–Wurt, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“At least it's safe to eat now.”
–Walter, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
“Finally, I can eat it!”
–Wanda, when examining Cooked Cactus Flesh.
Cooked Cactus Flesh is acquired by cooking Cactus Flesh on a Campfire, Fire Pit or Star. It restores the same Hunger as its raw form, but does not hurt the player's Health and restores 15 instead.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Despite the fact that the cooked cactus flesh does not harm the player upon consumption, and that the player has supposedly burned off the cactus spines by cooking it, it's sprite still depicts spines on it's surface.
Usage[edit | edit source]
Fillers cannot be Fruit.

'''Cactus Flesh''' is a [[Vegetable]] [[Food]] [[Item]] from the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' DLC. It can be harvested from [[Cactus]] at the cost of 6 {{Hp}}. Eating it raw will cost the player 5 [[Sanity]] and 3 [[Health]].

Cactus Flesh can be used as an ingredient in many [[Crock Pot]] recipes, but only [[Guacamole]] requires it specifically.

Feeding Cactus Flesh to [[Pig]]s will result in them producing [[Manure]].

Depending on what type it is, wearing [[Armor]] while harvesting a cactus can reduce damage down to 1 {{Hp}}. Since Cooked Cactus Flesh heals this amount, one can negate the harvesting damage entirely.

Since Cacti are unaffected by cold during [[Winter]], one can easily harvest Cactus Flesh and eat it cooked to regain [[Sanity]] lost from the long nights, or used in lieu of farmed vegetables in [[Crock Pot]] recipes. Cactus is easily renewable in the Desert biomes which makes it a good base location.

==Cooked Cactus Flesh==

'''Cooked Cactus Flesh''' is acquired by cooking Cactus Flesh on a [[Campfire]], [[Fire Pit]] or [[Star Caller's Staff|Star]]. It restores the same [[Hunger]] as its raw form, but does not hurt the player's Health and restores 15 {{Sanity}} instead.

== {{Trivia}}Trivia ==

* Despite the fact that the cooked cactus flesh does not harm the player upon consumption, and that the player has supposedly burned off the cactus spines by cooking it, it's sprite still depicts spines on it's surface.
